title: 长鑫科技 IPO 发行结果公布：网上投资者放弃认购 658 万股
content: Changxin Technology (688825.SH) completed its STAR Market IPO at 8.66 yuan per share, issuing 6.69 billion shares representing 10% of post-IPO total shares. The online lottery winning rate was approximately 0.47%. Online investors subscribed to 3.84 billion shares while abandoning 6.59 million shares; offline investors subscribed to 2.17 billion shares with 31,567 shares abandoned. Joint lead underwriters underwrote 6.62 million shares for 57.31 million yuan. Pre-greenshoe offering expenses totaled 281 million yuan.

title: Why Physical AI Is the Next Frontier | Applied Intuition
content: Applied Intuition, a decade-old software company powering intelligent machines across vehicles, defense, and industrial sectors, has launched Dana—a platform accelerating autonomous system development. Cofounders Qasar Younis and Peter Ludwig discuss physical AI with Marc Andreessen and Erik Torenberg, covering autonomous vehicles, humanoid robotics, world models, simulation, and AI infrastructure. They examine global competition and how reducing barriers to building physical AI could spur new products and companies.
source: The a16z Show - content+a16zpodcast@a16z.com (Erik Torenberg, Marc Andreessen, Qasar Younis, Peter Ludwig)

title: GPT-5.6 Sol上线四天被疑削弱：思考预算一度降至约八分之一，用户要求重跑评测 | 原文
content: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol, launched July 13, was accused of being quietly weakened four days after release. Users noted its "thinking budget" was allegedly reduced to about one-eighth of the original capacity. The model had initially received strong benchmarks and positive reviews. Critics complained users continued paying the same price for a degraded product without new evaluations being conducted. The complaints spread on social media, with one viral post mocking the alleged pattern of release, praise, then silent downgrading. User NIK highlighted the issue on X, prompting calls for benchmark re-testing.

title: 感觉又慢又贵：实测 Kimi-K3 编码能力
content: A developer benchmarked Kimi-K3 against DeepSeek-V4-Pro for AI coding tasks using two tools (Trae and Qoder) to build a desktop knowledge-base application with document import, indexing, Q&A, and history features. DeepSeek-V4-Pro completed tasks in 2-5 minutes at 0.5-1 RMB with consistent success. Kimi-K3 took 12-18 minutes at 8-9 RMB, showed roughly 10× higher token costs, and initially failed without a prompting framework. With a "Harness" framework, Kimi-K3 succeeded but was still slower and more expensive.
eventKey: 感觉又慢又贵-kimi-k3-编码能力
normalizedTitle: 感觉又慢又贵 实测 kimi-k3 编码能力

title: LWiAI Podcast #252 - GPT 5.6, Grok 4.5, Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion, AI 2040
content: Podcast episode 252 covers major AI developments from July 2026. OpenAI released GPT-5.6 and rebranded its desktop coding product as ChatGPT Work, amid disputed government involvement and jailbreak concerns. SpaceX AI launched low-cost Grok 4.5 with minimal safety documentation, while Meta released Muse Spark 1.1 and previewed Muse Video and Image—quickly withdrawing the latter after backlash over impersonation risks. Chinese open-source models surpassed 30% of OpenRouter usage. Other topics include Meta's cloud compute plans, US energy grid pressures, Anthropic's interpretability research, potential Chinese export restrictions, and a proposal for US-China coordination to slow AI progress.
source: Last Week in AI - Last Week in AI

title: Last Week in AI #251 - Mythos Back, Sonnet 5, Etched, LongCat
content: Anthropic re-released its Claude Fable 5 model after U.S. government discussions, adding cybersecurity classifiers and jailbreak frameworks, and launched Claude Sonnet 5 with improved coding performance and default cyber safeguards. Google introduced NotebookLM's TikTok-style video summaries and Nano Banana 2 Lite image generator. Etched advanced full-stack AI inference hardware with major funding. Baidu pursued AI chip IPO plans, Agility Robotics announced a SPAC merger, and DeepSeek expanded hiring. China released LongCat 2.0, an open-source mixture-of-experts model with advanced training techniques and long-horizon agent benchmarks.

title: Last Week in AI #250 - Mythos Mess, GPT 5.6-Sol, GLM 5.2
content: US government gatekeeping of frontier AI expands, with Anthropic permitted to release Mythos-5 to select entities, OpenAI launching GPT-5.6 "Sol" under restricted access, and Meta facing pressure for "voluntary" model reviews—signaling an emerging de facto licensing regime. Compute competition intensifies through custom chips from OpenAI, Amazon, and others. Open-source GLM 5.2 shows strong coding performance. Bipartisan workforce initiatives and AI safety roadmaps from DeepMind and Apollo advance, while corporate AI political spending and grassroots data-center protests highlight growing societal tensions.
source: Last Week in AI - Last Week in AI

title: LWiAI Podcast #249 - Fable 5 ban, SpaceX Cursor + IPO, OSS Aplenty
content: Anthropic restricted access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 following a US government order over alleged jailbreaks, sparking debate on policy consistency and export controls. SpaceX completed a $1.75 trillion IPO and acquired AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion, bolstering xAI's competitive position. Other developments include Anthropic's US data center leases backed by Google, leaked OpenAI financials showing significant losses, ChatGPT's market share dropping below 50%, new open-source releases from Moonshot, Qwen, and NVIDIA, DOJ support for xAI's unpermitted Memphis gas turbines, and a Munich court ruling Google liable for false AI Overview statements.
source: Last Week in AI - Last Week in AI

title: LWiAI Podcast #247 - Opus 4.8, MAI, Anthropic IPO, Minimax-M3
content: Andrey Kurenkov resumed the LWiAI podcast after a hiatus, releasing episode 247 covering major AI developments. Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.8 with dynamic workflows and filed for IPO at a $965 billion valuation after raising $65 billion. Microsoft introduced the Scout assistant and MAI models. Cognition raised $1 billion at $25 billion valuation. MiniMax-M3 outperformed GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro at lower cost. Trump signed an executive order for AI oversight. Policy coverage included tightened Nvidia export controls, Meta AI security exploitation, China's expert travel restrictions, and expanded biodefense initiatives.

title: 黄仁勋的“达链”闭环了
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has built a global supply chain, "Da Chain," spanning energy, chips, infrastructure, models, and applications. In Japan, Nvidia partnered with 44 companies including SoftBank and Sony to create Noetra, deploying 27,500 Rubin GPUs for physical AI training, with $2.4 billion in government funding. Japanese robot makers Fanuc, Yaskawa, and Kawasaki joined Nvidia's Cosmos alliance, unifying development standards. SK Hynix supplies 70% of HBM4 memory under a binding agreement through 2030. Taiwan handles manufacturing and assembly with 150 suppliers. However, core U.S. customers OpenAI and Anthropic are developing their own chips, threatening Nvidia's foundation despite record revenues of $215.9 billion in FY2026.

title: 时代变了，清华的分可以去上公安大学了 | 原文
content: Police academies in China are seeing admission scores surge to rival elite universities. In Jiangsu, China People's Public Security University minimum score reached 652, just below Peking and Tsinghua universities, surpassing top 985 institutions. Zhejiang Police College also reported rises: overall minimum up 15 points, with Zhoushan male applicants in criminal technology jumping 26 points. The trend signals shifting student priorities toward stable government careers over traditional academic prestige.

title: 谷歌密谋「焊死」Gemini，神秘芯片曝光，能效碾压TPU十倍
content: Google is developing "Frozen v2," a specialized server chip that permanently etches parts of Gemini's architecture into silicon rather than running models on general-purpose hardware. Expected to deploy by 2028, it could achieve 6-10x better energy efficiency per watt than Google's latest TPUs. Unlike an earlier abandoned concept that would have burned in model weights, this version only hardwires the computational blueprint, allowing weight updates. The move reflects severe compute shortages forcing Google Cloud to reject customers and pay SpaceX $920 million monthly for GPU access through 2029. The bet risks obsolescence if Gemini's architecture changes significantly.
eventKey: 谷歌密谋「焊死」gemini-神秘芯片曝光-能效碾压tpu十倍

title: 做了一个完全本地的语音转文字工具，不想只做一个 Whisper GUI
content: OpenASR is a local, offline speech-to-text tool supporting multiple ASR models beyond Whisper, including Qwen3-ASR, SenseVoice, FireRed, Dolphin, and X-ASR. It features unified inference engine, model marketplace, real-time subtitles, global voice input, CLI, and OpenAI-compatible local API. Built in Rust and open-sourced under Apache-2.0, it targets macOS Apple Silicon and Windows x64 with GPU acceleration.

title: Kimi K3爆火，GPU先扛不住了
content: Chinese AI startup Moonshot AI paused new subscriptions for its Kimi K3 model less than a week after launch due to GPU compute shortages, prioritizing existing users. K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-source MoE model, ranked third globally on Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index and gained significant developer attention for coding and agent tasks. However, experts note reliability issues in complex professional tasks. The incident highlights intensifying competition in inference infrastructure and compute capacity as a bottleneck for large-scale AI deployment.
eventKey: kimi-k3爆火-gpu先扛不住了
normalizedTitle: kimi k3爆火 gpu先扛不住了

title: WAIC 2026终极盘点：15大核心趋势，看透AI下半场
content: WAIC 2026 highlighted 15 key AI trends: AI phones evolving into agent-native devices; AI glasses and headphones focusing on lightweight, specialized functions; agents becoming central across industries as "AI colleagues"; Chinese tech giants building comprehensive platforms; domestic large models approaching 3T parameters; AI chip firms emphasizing full localization; "super nodes" enabling massive compute clusters; Token replacing FLOPS as the new metric; robots entering practical deployment despite overheating issues; embodied intelligence advancing through brain-eye-hand integration; dexterous hands attracting major investment; physical AI data scarcity driving collection infrastructure; and AI accelerating healthcare and scientific research.

title: DeepSeek V4「满血版」最快明天发布
content: DeepSeek V4 will launch as early as tomorrow in Flash and Pro tiers. Some users already have gray-scale access, identifiable by first-person chain-of-thought responses. Performance reportedly nears Opus 4.8, with coding approaching GPT-5.6 Sol, enhanced Agent capabilities, and improved 3D/SVG generation, though iteration rounds exceed Fable 5 and it likely trails Kimi K3. Pricing is significantly lower: Pro outputs cost $0.87 per million tokens off-peak, $1.74 peak; Flash costs $0.28/$0.56. Peak-valley billing is introduced, with very low cache-hit input prices. Older deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner models retire July 24.
eventKey: deepseek-v4「满血版」最快明天发布
normalizedTitle: deepseek v4「满血版」最快明天发布

title: 为自己的 agent 支持通过 iLink API 链接微信
content: A developer implemented WeChat integration for the open-source tea_agent project using iLink API. The implementation included creating a remote interface similar to Telegram's. A detailed Chinese markdown tutorial was produced, documenting tea_agent's compatibility, online search capabilities, and implementation process. The tutorial targets programmers and includes exported PDF documentation with user inputs, tool calls, and AI messages.
